name: the-great-bridge
description: David McCullough's The Great Bridge — engineering and history narrative recounting the building of the Brooklyn Bridge, from John Roebling's vision to Washington's heroic determination through caisson disease and wire fraud to the bridge as a monument to American ingenuity
useCase: Understand the story of the Brooklyn Bridge, learn from the Roeblings' determination, explore 19th century engineering and corruption
knownRisks: The book was first published in 1972 — some historical interpretations may have evolved
McCullough is a narrative historian who prioritizes story over academic analysis
The technical aspects of bridge building may be dense for some readers
